title: GGUF conversion + structural sanity, pruned v4 judge (judge-only)
llamacpp_build: version: 1 (8f114a9) built with GNU 13.3.0 for Linux aarch64
b9180_floor_note: PKG4-01 pins >=b9180 (MTP conversion support for Qwen3.5/3.6); recorded build is past it
mtp_deviation_note: The first conversion attempt (block_count=41, including the MTP/nextn layer) produced a GGUF that FAILED TO LOAD in llama-server: 'check_tensor_dims: tensor blk.40.ffn_gate_inp.weight has wrong shape; expected 2048,224, got 2048,256' -- because Phase 26's prune surgery deliberately left the MTP/nextn layer (and shared_expert.*) at the original 256 experts while pruning the 40 trunk layers to 224, and GGUF's expert_count metadata is a single GLOBAL field llama.cpp's loader enforces uniformly across every block. Root-caused and fixed by re-converting with the officially supported 'convert_hf_to_gguf.py --no-mtp' flag (confirmed in ~/llama.cpp/conversion/qwen.py's _Qwen35MtpMixin: drops all mtp.* tensors, sets block_count=num_hidden_layers exactly -- no mixed-count block). This GGUF has NO MTP/speculative-decoding head; block_count is 40, not 41. Verified safe: none of this phase's eval harnesses (_pkg_gguf_eval_run.sh) exercise MTP/speculative decoding, and the corrected Q8_0 was smoke-tested (loads, listens, real generation succeeds) before use. scripts/eval4_ext_gguf_convert.sh was extended with an optional 4th 'extra_convert_arg' positional and the block-count formula now branches on --no-mtp.

size_vs_projection.note: 33.6 is a PROJECTION and is recorded here only to close it out against the measurement; the measured value is the only one any downstream doc or card may cite. The measured Q8 (30.38 GiB) came in SMALLER than the linear-scaling projection -- the --no-mtp GGUF excludes the MTP/nextn head entirely (never counted separately in the projection's linear-scaling math), on top of the projection not accounting for shared-expert/DeltaNet tensors being fixed (non-expert-count-scaling) overhead.

v3_comparison.note: recorded per the 2026-07-17 user directive: size-vs-v3 is a NOTE, not a ship gate
